fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Minnesota Pharmacist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacist? Here's whether your license transfers to Minnesota - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacist license reciprocity minnesota, how to transfer pharmacist license to minnesota, pharmacist compact minnesota, minnesota pharmacist endorsement application

board.name: Minnesota Board of Pharmacy
board.mailing_address: 2829 University Ave SE, Suite 530, Minneapolis, MN 55414-3251

license_types.title: RPh, PharmD
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacist in Minnesota.

steps.title: Create Account and Submit Application
steps.description: Access the Minnesota Board of Pharmacy Online Services Portal, register as a new user, and complete the online Pharmacist Application, paying the non-refundable application fee.

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts
steps.description: Have your ACPE-accredited pharmacy school send official transcripts directly to the Minnesota Board of Pharmacy.

steps.title: Complete Criminal Background Check (CBC)
steps.description: Follow the Board's instructions to complete a criminal background check, which typically involves fingerprinting.

steps.title: Pass or Transfer Exam Scores
steps.description: If applying by examination, register with NABP to take and pass the NAPLEX and the Minnesota MPJE. If applying by endorsement, ensure your existing NAPLEX and MPJE (or equivalent) scores are transferred or verified by the Board.

steps.title: Await Licensure
steps.description: Ensure all required documentation and fees are submitted and verified by the Board of Pharmacy. The Board will issue your license once all requirements are met and processed.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NAPLEX and MPJE.

provisional_text: Some states offer an intern or provisional license for graduates actively accumulating required hours or waiting to take the NAPLEX.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

faqs.question: How much does a Minnesota pharmacist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies by state. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is the MPJE required in all states?
faqs.answer: Almost all states require passing their state-specific Multistate Pharmacy Jurisprudence Examination (MPJE) to verify knowledge of local pharmacy laws.

faqs.question: Does Minnesota have reciprocity for pharmacists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacist practice in Minnesota. To work in Minnesota, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacist license to Minnesota?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Minnesota while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

hero.title: Become a Pharmacist in Minnesota
hero.dek: To apply for a Pharmacist license in Minnesota, you must use the Minnesota Board of Pharmacy Online Services Portal to submit your application and supporting documents.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal

official_links.portal_name: Minnesota Board of Pharmacy Online Services Portal
official_links.registration_steps: Access the Minnesota Board of Pharmacy Online Services Portal, click 'Register' for new users, and complete the account creation process to begin your application.
